title: Reading and Use of English – Part 1
description: Read the text below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap.

content.text: For centuries, the seasonal disappearance of birds was a mystery that (0) baffled early naturalists. Some even proposed that birds spent the winter underwater or flew to the moon. Today, we know that migration is a complex biological feat, driven by the need to find (1) food sources and suitable breeding grounds. These journeys often involve crossing vast oceans and deserts, requiring incredible endurance. Navigational accuracy is (2) to their survival, and researchers have discovered that birds rely on a sophisticated array of internal compasses. Some species (3) use of the Earth’s magnetic field, while others appear to navigate by the stars. Environmental cues, such as the position of the sun, also (4) a significant role in helping them maintain their course. However, human activity is increasingly putting these incredible voyages at (5) through habitat loss and light pollution. Conservationists are now working to (6) international agreements to protect vital stopover sites along major flyways. Protecting these areas is essential to (7) that future generations can still witness this spectacular natural event. Despite modern tracking technology, many aspects of how young birds navigate for the first time remain (8) to debate, reminding us that nature still holds many secrets that scientists are only just beginning to uncover.

example.explanation: “Baffled” is the most appropriate verb to describe a scientific or intellectual puzzle that people cannot solve.

questions.explanation: “Plentiful food sources” is a standard ecological collocation. The others are used for people or liquids.
“Paramount to survival” is a fixed formal expression meaning of the greatest importance.
“Make use of” is a fixed idiomatic phrase meaning to utilize something.
“Play a role” is the standard collocation for contributing to a result.
“At risk” is the correct fixed prepositional phrase in this context.
“Forge an agreement” is a common collocation used for creating formal diplomatic ties.
“Ensure” means to make certain that something will happen. “Assure” is used for people.
“Open to debate” is a fixed idiom meaning something is not yet certain or agreed upon.
